old_claim.note: Electronics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Power electronics and electrical integration help deploy solar generation, but photovoltaic conversion itself does not require later electronics as a hard prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: IEA solar technology page reviewing solar PV and solar power deployment as energy technologies.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports electronics and grid integration as deployment context for solar generation, not the physical photovoltaic conversion dependency.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected edge keeps electronics relevant for scaling solar systems while leaving semiconductors and electricity as the stronger technical dependencies.

ontology_before: The graph treated electronics as a direct enabling dependency for solar power.
ontology_after: Electronics is now a commercial/scaling dependency for deployed solar systems.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant added in 2026-06-07-energy-edge-source-scope.json to lock the corrected Energy Systems edge semantics.
would_reject_if: A more specific primary or textbook source showed the original dependency type was globally required for the scoped technology.
The dependent node were narrowed to a later industrial product where the old prerequisite is truly mandatory.
A future source-check splits the node and moves this edge onto a more precise downstream technology.
short_reason: Electronics scales deployed solar systems but is not the photovoltaic conversion mechanism.
